_meta.description: Case-insensitive regex patterns identifying NCBI Taxonomy administrative containers to skip during taxonomic tree construction. Nodes whose scientific_name matches any pattern are skipped; their sequences escalate to the next valid ancestor.
_meta.policy: Add patterns here as they are identified in the registry census. Document the rationale for each pattern in the 'description' field of its block.

patterns.description: NCBI 'unclassified X' containers - unresolved taxonomic position. Covers 'unclassified Caudoviricetes', 'unclassified Begomovirus', 'unclassified viruses', etc.
Formal Latin variant of 'unclassified'. Covers 'Caudoviricetes incertae sedis', 'Viruses incertae sedis', etc. Used by the ICTV for clades without a defined phylogenetic position.
Environmental/metagenomic samples with no characterized isolate. Usually single leaves per accession.
Organisms detected via sequencing but never cultured. Typically metagenomic.
NCBI submissions without identification. Rare in viruses, common in other domains.
Deposit of findings from Shi et al. - invertebrate virome. ShiM-2016 alone has 883 species under 'unclassified RNA viruses ShiM-2016'. Other papers may have similar patterns - add as identified.
Containers of clinical/outbreak isolates. E.g. 'Norovirus isolates', 'Sapovirus isolates'. These are below-species groupings, not taxonomic classes.
Serological subgroups of norovirus and similar. 'Norovirus genogroup 1 isolates', etc.
Circulating recombinant forms of HIV-1. Clinical subdivision, not taxonomic.
Catch-all for entries with malformed names. E.g. 'Influenza A virus with incomplete names'.
Literal NCBI 'mixed subtypes' container for Influenza.

rank_blacklist.description: Administrative taxonomic ranks indicating below-species subdivision (clinical serology, not biology). Nodes with these ranks are skipped regardless of name.
